_comment: Static model placement across data centers. Placement decisions are constrained by GPU memory capacity. Opus 70B (140GB/replica) can only be hosted at the two largest facilities. Vision 13B is concentrated at coastal DCs where enterprise multimodal demand is highest.
_methodology: Each DC reserves GPU memory for model weights. Placement optimized for coverage and latency, not grid cost — this intentional tension between placement and grid optimization is a key finding.

dc_hammond_il.note: Largest facility (80MW). Only Midwest DC hosting Opus 70B — NYC/Boston users needing Opus must route here despite distance.

dc_plano_tx.note: Mid-size facility (30MW). Flash and Sonnet only — insufficient memory headroom for Opus 70B without crowding out inference capacity.

dc_chester_va.note: Smallest facility (28MW). Flash and Sonnet only. Serves DC/Virginia corridor for low-latency Flex 1.

dc_weehawken_nj.note: NYC metro (50MW). Vision 13B hosted here for enterprise multimodal demand. No Opus 70B — memory would crowd out Flash inference capacity for the dense NYC request volume.

dc_eagle_mountain_ut.note: Second largest facility (75MW). Full model catalog. West Coast overflow for Opus 70B when San Jose is at capacity.

dc_san_jose_ca.note: Bay Area (40MW). No Opus 70B — 140GB replica would consume 17% of total GPU memory, crowding out the high-volume Flash inference traffic on CAISO's cheap solar grid.
